codeReview.summary: Verification-only feature — no code changes were made. All 5 claims confirmed independently: (1) Scene library has zero layout helper definitions (grep confirmed). (2) Scene library has no content-aware code — no monospace, chars_per_line, char_width, TextRun, PathCmd, Content::Text, or Content::Path references. (3) Core has all 5 layout helpers (byte_to_line_col at line 810, layout_mono_lines at 835, line_bytes_for_run at 897, scroll_runs at 909, bytes_to_shaped_glyphs at 936 in scene_state.rs). (4) Scene library exports are exclusively geometric primitives (NodeId, Node, Content with FillRect/Glyphs/Image, ShapedGlyph, SceneWriter/Reader, etc.). (5) Test crate has correct copies of all 5 helpers using TestLayoutRun struct — algorithms are identical to core, structural differences (extra advance/axis_hash fields) are expected since the test crate cannot import from the core service binary. Worker ran full test suite and build successfully. Worker noted a stale TextRun::glyph_count comment at scene/lib.rs:967 — my review finds no such comment in the current codebase (line 967 correctly references Content::Glyphs), so this issue was likely already resolved by another feature.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The refactor-worker skill procedure has no verification-only pathway. Step 3 'Execute the Move' doesn't apply when the feature is purely a verification task. The worker correctly adapted by skipping that step, and noted this in skillFeedback.suggestedChanges. The skill could benefit from an explicit verification-only branch that skips directly to 'Verify Completeness' when the feature description indicates no code changes are expected.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Worker transcript shows the worker ran all verification steps (grep, build, tests) but had no 'Execute the Move' step since the code was already correct. handoff.skillFeedback.suggestedChanges explicitly calls this out.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker's discoveredIssues noted a stale TextRun::glyph_count comment at scene/lib.rs:967 that does not exist in the current codebase. The comment at line 967 correctly references Content::Glyphs. This suggests either the comment was already fixed by a parallel feature (cleanup-documentation), or the worker misread the line. Minor accuracy gap in the handoff — discovered issues should be verified against current HEAD before reporting.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.discoveredIssues[0] says 'Stale comment in scene/lib.rs line 967 references TextRun::glyph_count'. Grep for 'TextRun' in scene/lib.rs returns zero matches. Line 967 reads: '/// `glyph_count` is the number of glyphs expected (from `Content::Glyphs`).'

summary: PASS. All 5 verification claims confirmed independently. Scene library is fully content-agnostic with zero layout helpers. Core contains all 5 helpers. Test crate copies use identical algorithms with expected structural differences (TestLayoutRun vs LayoutRun). No code quality issues found. Worker performed thorough verification including full build and test suite. One minor handoff accuracy issue: a reported stale comment does not exist in the current codebase.
